title: proofbundle conformance vector (case.json)
description: Fundament F1 — the ONE machine-readable vector format for the whole conformance corpus. The offline runner (run_conformance.py), the cross-format comparator (cross_format.py) and the Python<->Rust differential (crosscheck.py) all consume THIS format; there is no second vector format in the repo. A case declares what it proves (expected) AND, optionally, its cross-format identity (crossFormatId) so the SAME logical scenario expressed in different encodings can be held to ONE common-vocabulary verdict (conformance/common_vocabulary.py). Additive: 3.4.0/3.5.0/3.6.0 add new expected axes and kinds here, never a parallel schema.

caseId.description: Stable, human-readable id, unique across the corpus.

kind.description: Dispatch key for the runner. New kinds are added additively (fail-closed: an unknown kind is a per-case FAIL, never a silent skip). outcome_relation (3.4.0, WP-B) mirrors decision_relation on the action-outcome verify path. relation_statement (3.5.0, WP-A) is the standalone relation-statement/v0.1 verify path (`relation-statement verify`).

input.description: Relative fixture filename inside the case directory (confined; no traversal, no absolute path).

pub.description: decision_relation only: relative filename holding the base64 public key.

relatedPubs.description: WP-A (3.4.0): base64 issuer keys position-paired with `related` (i-th key for the i-th target; empty string = same-key/--pub). Enables cross-issuer chains verified under relation_signer.

policy.description: Optional relative filename of a trust policy fixture.

verifyArgs.description: Extra CLI verify args drawn from a small allowlist (no file/network flags).

requiresAnchorsExtra.description: The deciding rejection reason needs the [anchors] extra; skipped honestly on a base install unless --require-anchors.

expected.description: Fail-closed declaration of the verdict. At least one axis MUST be pinned. The runner asserts every pinned axis; an unpinned axis is not compared. exitCode/exitClass, lineage and policyVerdict map onto the F1 common vocabulary.

exitCode.description: CLI verify exit-code contract: 0 VERIFIED, 1 CRYPTO_FAIL, 2 MALFORMED, 3 POLICY_UNMET.

exitClass.description: Named exit class (alternative to the numeric exitCode; the F1 vocabulary maps between them).

lineage.description: relation/v0.1 lineage state. null == NOT_EVALUATED (same observable fact).

policyVerdict.description: Decision predicate verdict, when the case exercises one.

crossFormatId.description: Cross-format identity. Cases sharing a crossFormatId are the SAME logical scenario in different encodings/levels and MUST agree on the F1 common-vocabulary verdict (cross_format.py enforces this — structurally closing the 3.4.0 F3 'circular' finding).

specRefs.description: Pointers to the spec/design sections this case pins.
